picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: A free summer festival on the waterfront Marineterrein — food, music, a swim spot and design across the old naval yard.

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: VBX Summer Weekend
picks.blurb: Rey Colino, Paquita Gordon, Muallem, Ion Ludwig and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: PRNCPTL DOES AMSTERDAM - DAY & NIGHT
picks.blurb: CRYME, DJ Fuckoff, SALOME, Slimfit and more at Lofi.

picks.title: Man in the Mirror
picks.blurb: Michael Jackson tribute with liveband, dancers, iconic moves and costume recreating the King of Pop's greatest hits.
picks.why: MJ's biggest hits live on stage

picks.title: Love on the Canals
picks.when: Fri 3 Jul – Sun 5 Jul (opens Fri, runs until 27 Sep)
picks.blurb: 30th anniversary exhibition: the history of Amsterdam's Pride boat parade with photos, art, personal stories.
picks.why: New opening during Pride, historical & visual
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Pulitzer-winning photojournalism exhibition spanning global issues, conflict, and human stories. Essential viewing for documentary lovers.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: TREK – Street Food Festival
picks.blurb: Open-air food festival with global street food, beer garden, live music & disco evening.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Festival TREK official site

picks.title: Bostheater
picks.blurb: Unique outdoor theatre venue in Amsterdam's forest with theatre, live music and family shows all summer. Book ahead—sells out quickly.
picks.why: Outdoor theatre in nature with family shows

picks.title: Oatly x Radio Radio Pop-Up
picks.blurb: Relaxed outdoor summer hangout with DJs, live radio broadcasts, Oatly drinks, and festival atmosphere through August.
picks.why: Summer outdoor hangout with DJs and events

picks.title: Openluchttheater Vondelpark
picks.blurb: Free outdoor theatre, music, dance and cabaret performances all summer in Amsterdam's iconic park. Bring a picnic.

picks.title: De Hortus Botanicus Summer Evenings
picks.blurb: Botanical garden open late with live music, guided tours, and terrace drinks amid blooming gardens and tropical greenhouses.
picks.why: Botanical garden with live music and sunset views

picks.title: Garlic Festival at Restaurant VRR
picks.blurb: Garlic-focused market with live music and stalls, followed by optional three-course garlic dinner with vegetarian options.
picks.why: Unique garlic-themed market and dinner experience

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Japanese maritime history and cultural narratives through the museum's collection of historical shipping artifacts.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore centuries of Dutch-Japanese cultural exchange through lacquerware, ceremonial swords, and decorative screens.

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Personal exploration of identity and self-expression through photographs, self-portraits, and childhood objects.

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End hit musical based on true story of Henry Fraser showing how one moment changes life, performed on Carré's iconic stage.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: Spinoza
picks.blurb:
Amsterdam, 17th century. 
 A city that sees itself as a safe haven. 
 A Portuguese-Jewish refugee community. 
 Three children. One family. 
 About Spinoza
 Bent
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Monne
picks.blurb: Mediterranean restaurant with waterside terrace and boat dock offering seasonal cuisine with relaxed escape-from-city vibes.
picks.why: Waterfront Mediterranean dining with boat views

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant with Cairo-inspired dishes and Mediterranean influences designed for sharing with family and friends.
picks.why: Egyptian sharing cuisine, perfect for family weekend lunch

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Amsterdam Gay Men's Chorus Concert
picks.blurb: 100+ singers and dancers from Amsterdam's queer community performing Queen, Stromae, and Britney Spears.

picks.title: The Thing
picks.blurb:
A science fiction horror about paranoia and betrayal
 In John Carpenter's The Thing (1982), a group of American scientists at an isolated research station in th

picks.title: Agatha's Almanac
picks.blurb:
Life on the farm in pictures
 In the documentary Agatha's Almanac , director Amalie Atkins follows her 90-year-old aunt Agatha on the old family farm in Manitob

picks.title: Boom Chicago Comedy Festival
picks.blurb: International stand-up, improv & sketch comedy with visiting headliners.

picks.title: Jazz @ H'ART Museum
picks.blurb:
Wonderful jazz in a summer setting
 Jazz lovers can count on a varied program performed by talents from the Amsterdam Conservatory. From energetic saxophone set

picks.title: Wonderfeel Classical Music Festival
picks.blurb: 400 classical musicians, 100+ shows: baroque to minimal, jazz to pop, in 18th-century castle grounds.

picks.title: StandOut's Arabic Comedy Showcase Show
picks.blurb:
Let the good vibes roll
 Expect a high-energy atmosphere with a rotating cast of rising stars and seasoned pros, all sharing the stage to deliver their best mat

picks.title: Filippo & Aruth - Black & White Cuphead
picks.blurb:
Scala food bar theater
 Scala offers you a unique evening out: combine dining with 20-minute performances. The shows span various genres, from cabaret to music

picks.title: Chef's Evening
picks.blurb:
Dining in a unique setting
 Sharing is connecting. The dishes are festively presented on large platters, so that you enjoy pure flavors together with your table

picks.title: STRAAT Collectie
picks.venue: STRAAT Museum – Street art en graffiti in Amsterdam
picks.blurb:
Museum for street art and graffiti
 We focus on curating a collection that truly reflects the international street art movement. We do this by recruiting artist

picks.title: Bottomless Brunch
picks.blurb:
Bottomless brunch
 For groups larger than 10 people, there is a special offer available. This deal is valid at Cut Throat's location at Beursplein 5. The highli

picks.title: Lunchconcerten in de Westerkerk
picks.blurb:
Lunch concerts 
 For many years, the Westerkerk has hosted weekly lunch concerts in which the Duyschot organ excels under the hands of young top talents. Baroqu

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:
Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke
 Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke returns to the Kleine Zaal, where he continues his remarkable recital tradition. Praised by

picks.title: Lunchtime organ concerts at Waterlooplein
picks.blurb:
A moment of music in the middle of the day
 Step inside the Moses and Aaron Church and leave the hustle and bustle of the city behind for a moment. These luncht

picks.title: Julidans
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's premier contemporary dance festival showcasing international and Dutch choreographers across multiple venues.

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb:
Art, craft and design
 Museum Market takes place every month, every 3rd Sunday of the month (in October the 4th Sunday of the month). Between the trees near the

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: Art Amsterdam Spui
picks.blurb:
Discover art that truly touches you
 At Art Amsterdam Spui, artists present and sell their work themselves — straight from the studio and in direct contact with

picks.title: BOPS queer. pop. party
picks.blurb:
BOPS queer. pop. party
 Lineup: Resident DJs TMORGZ and Absoluut + various guest DJs
 BOPS is a space for the queer community and allies to dance the night away

picks.title: Pax Romana — Five Year Anniversary
picks.blurb: Queer techno party celebrates five years with extended marathon of high-energy rave and techno cuts.

picks.title: Rave Tribe Festival 2026
picks.blurb: House, trance, acid and oldskool rave classics with Lucien Foort, Marco V, ROOG, Erick E and more at industrial festival venue.
picks.why: Full lineup of Dutch house legends

picks.title: RAUM invites Maricas x Bass for Dykes
picks.blurb: Queer electronic music party with techno, electro and acid from Maricas and Bass for Dykes collectives.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Ten Katemarkt
picks.blurb: A proper everyday neighbourhood market off the tourist track — heaped produce, street food and Oud-West locals doing the shop.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.

picks.title: Kinderdijk Windmills
picks.blurb: Nineteen 18th-century windmills lined along the polders — the postcard Dutch landscape, a UNESCO site you can walk or cycle.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.
